name: Bergout, Ichantes, and Jaupins MTB Tour
name_original: De Bedous jusqu'au pic de Soum
description: This difficult 16.1-mile (25.8 km) mountain biking tour in the Pyrenees National Park offers challenging ascents and thrilling descents.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Bedous are set within the Aspe Valley, a region characterized by the towering Pyrenees Mountains and diverse terrain. The landscape features dense forests, the flowing Gave d'Aspe river, and dramatic geological formations like gorges and cirques. This area provides a varied environment for mountain biking, from challenging mountain ascents to scenic valley routes. Best mountain bike trails around Bedous The most popular mountain bike trail is Bergout, Ichantes, and Jaupins MTB Tour, a 16.0 miles (25.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 39 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain and expansive mountain views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Borde de Bouhaben singletrack – Le Poeys loop from Accous, a difficult 16.3 miles (26.2 km) path. This route features challenging singletrack sections through varied mountain terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Lescun – Lescun loop from Accous, a 15.7 miles (25.3 km) trail leading through the dramatic Cirque de Lescun, often completed in about 2 hours 45 minutes. Mountain biking around Bedous is defined by the Pyrenees Mountains, the Aspe Valley, and dense forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy valley rides to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Bedous are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 250 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Bedous's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Bedous?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Bedous, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes are set within the stunning Aspe Valley, characterized by the towering Pyrenees Mountains and varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Bedous?
A: The terrain around Bedous is highly varied, ranging from challenging mountain ascents to scenic valley routes. You'll encounter dense forests, the flowing Gave d'Aspe river, and dramatic geological formations like gorges and cirques. The Pyrenees Mountains provide a dramatic backdrop and contribute to significant elevation changes on many routes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Bedous?
A: Yes, there are trails suitable for beginners. For an easier ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/29294814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bedous – Church of Athas loop from Accous&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers about 17.8 km with a moderate elevation gain, making it a good option for those new to mountain biking or looking for a less strenuous outing.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find plenty of challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e965014828/bergout-ichantes-and-jaupins-mtb-tour" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bergout, Ichantes, and Jaupins MTB Tour&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 25.8 km route with significant elevation gain and expansive mountain views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39165508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Houratate Pass. – Church of Athas loop from Osse-en-Aspe&lt;/a&gt;, which features substantial climbs over 14.8 km.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Bedous area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Bedous are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24644001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lescun – Lescun loop from Accous&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the dramatic Cirque de Lescun, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/29294814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bedous – Church of Athas loop from Accous&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails in Bedous?
A: The trails often pass by or offer views of stunning natural features. You might encounter the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1041664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montagnon Lake&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3123830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanchèse Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or dramatic geological formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6312141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges de Lourdios&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its dense forests and the picturesque Gave d'Aspe river.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks accessible by mountain bike near Bedous?
A: Absolutely. The region offers several panoramic viewpoints and significant landmarks. You can find breathtaking views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3461039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic de Soum de Bergout&lt;/a&gt;. The famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marie-Blanque Pass&lt;/a&gt;, an emblematic passage of the Tour de France, also provides remarkable scenery and challenging climbs.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Bedous?
A: The mountain bike trails in Bedous are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning Pyrenean landscapes, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels. Many appreciate the immersive natural experience within the Aspe Valley.

Q: Is Bedous a good destination for mountain biking with family?
A: Bedous offers options for family-friendly mountain biking, especially on easier routes that follow the valley floor or less steep forest paths. The diverse range of trails means you can select routes appropriate for various ages and skill levels within your family. Always check the difficulty and elevation profile before heading out.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Bedous?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Bedous is generally from spring through autumn. During these seasons, the weather is more stable, and the trails are typically clear of snow. Summer offers warm conditions, while spring and autumn provide cooler temperatures and vibrant scenery. Winter riding can be challenging due to snow in higher elevations.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Cirque de Lescun?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24644001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lescun – Lescun loop from Accous&lt;/a&gt; is a popular mountain bike trail that leads through the dramatic Cirque de Lescun. This route offers stunning views of the vast glacial cirque and the surrounding peaks, providing a memorable riding experience.

Q: Can I find singletrack sections on the mountain bike trails in Bedous?
A: Yes, for those who enjoy technical riding, there are routes featuring challenging singletrack sections. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26205263" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Borde de Bouhaben singletrack – Le Poeys loop from Accous&lt;/a&gt; is known for its varied mountain terrain and engaging singletrack portions.
tips: Great route through the Aspée Valley in France. It is divided into 3 very hard descents and 3 ascents, where the first 2 coincide in part in the ascent. Authentic MTB.
Nice trail to reach that point that offers amazing views !

faqs.question: Is the Bergout, Ichantes, and Jaupins MTB Tour suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this mountain bike tour is classified as difficult or hard. It features significant elevation gain, technical ascents, slanted passages, steps, and demanding descents with pebbly terrain and roots. It's best suited for experienced mountain bikers looking for an intense challenge.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike tour?
faqs.answer: The tour offers varied terrain, including tarmac ascents, technical forest paths, and a steep, pebbly ridge section with steps. You'll encounter short, technical climbs and two top-class descents, notably the 'dream singletrack' of Ichantes, which can be long and demanding with roots and blind bends.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The tour is renowned for its beautiful scenery, starting with a bucolic ambiance and a pleasant ascent through the Aulet Valley. From the ridge sections and during descents, you can enjoy 'a knockout view!' The Bergout area is also a paraglider take-off point, offering panoramic vistas. Don't miss the Pic de Soum de Bergout - incredible view.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to ride the Bergout, Ichantes, and Jaupins MTB Tour?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal details aren't provided, mountain biking in the Pyrénées-Atlantiques region is generally best during late spring, summer, and early autumn (May to October) when the weather is more stable and trails are clear of snow. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Bergout, Ichantes, and Jaupins MTB Tour?
faqs.answer: The tour starts near Bedous. While specific parking locations aren't detailed, you would typically look for parking in or around the village of Bedous, which serves as a common starting point for routes in the Aspe Valley. Public transport options to Bedous might also be available.

faqs.question: Is the Bergout, Ichantes, and Jaupins MTB Tour dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The tour is located within the Pyrenees National Park. While dogs are generally allowed on a leash in some areas of national parks, specific regulations can vary. It's always best to check the current rules for the Pyrenees National Park regarding dogs on trails, especially given that sections of this route are shared with hikers.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this tour in the Pyrenees National Park?
faqs.answer: As this tour is located within the Pyrenees National Park, there are generally no entrance fees for accessing the park itself. However, specific activities or certain protected zones might have regulations. For mountain biking, you typically don't need a permit, but always respect park rules, especially regarding shared trails and wildlife.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike tour connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other trails. Notably, it covers 55% of the 'Tour Bergout Ichantes Jaupins VTT asp31' and also intersects with parts of the 'Trail d'Ourdinse asp18' (27%) and the 'Tour de la Borde de Bouhaben VTT asp30' (25%). You'll also find smaller sections overlapping with trails like 'Chemin Napoléon VTC' and 'Chemins du Gave VTC asp13'.

faqs.question: What should I be aware of regarding shared trails?
faqs.answer: From the Col de Bergout onwards, the trail is shared with hikers. It's crucial to exercise caution, reduce your speed, and give way to pedestrians to ensure safety for everyone enjoying the route. Always be respectful of other trail users.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' classification, riders should be prepared for technical sections, steep descents with pebbly terrain and roots, and blind bends. Always wear appropriate safety gear, carry sufficient water and supplies, and ensure your mountain bike is in excellent condition. It's advisable to ride with others and inform someone of your route and expected return time.

faqs.question: Is the tour a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The 'Bergout, Ichantes, and Jaupins MTB Tour' is designed as a circular route, allowing you to start and finish at the same point, typically Bedous, after completing the full circuit of Bergout, Ichantes, and Jaupins.
